plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-322 RP10.P01.M08.S06-RP10.P02.M07.S06 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-322 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP10.P01 model phase (M08 Hermes tail, M09 Claude review packet, M10 closeout head) and opens the RP10.P02 service foundation (M00-M07: service entrypoint contracts, request normalization, tenant/matter/permission/audit prechecks, primary/secondary paths, state transition enforcement, idempotency key handling, lock acquisition rules, persistence boundaries, validation error mapping, review/approval routing, blocked-claim outputs, rollback/retry behavior, unit tests, integration smoke cases) across eleven sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H10), Claude review packet (C10), and CP00-323 handoff, without opening intake, conflict check, waiver, engagement, or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-322 is a descriptor-only intake-core foundation pack (RP10, 150 units, range RP10.P01.M08.S06-RP10.P02.M07.S06) that closes the RP10.P01 model phase (M08 Hermes tail, M09 Claude review packet, M10 closeout head) and opens the RP10.P02 service foundation (M00-M07 heads). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level production_ready claim is honestly FALSE (manifest and command-evidence both status=implemented_pending_review, production_ready=false); the design correctly separates production_ready_candidate (local validation passed: coverage.valid && foundation.valid) from the false pack-level production_ready, so there is no overclaim. Plan binding is consistent and self-checked: closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-322 (unit_count 150, risk C, deviation_from_plan=false, source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65...) matches the registry constants and the prompt's deliverable_distribution (impl 70 / ui 23 / contract 8 / security_audit 16 / claude_review 5 / failure_recovery 10 / test 18 = 150) and micro_title_distribution; all 11 required section-row sets and per-micro/per-phase counts (P01=14, P02=136) reconcile. Descriptor-only boundaries are enforced by validators.js (every row descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false / customer_safe_errors_only=true / raw_payload_included=false; no-leak guards for permission-decision, audit-hint, validation-error, blocked-claim, and lock-token; cross_tenant_access_allowed=false; matter_trace_required=true; no state writes, idempotency persistence, runtime locks, db writes, rollback/retry, test runtime, or integration-smoke runtime). No real data and no product-state writes: descriptor source (registry/service/validators) performs no fs/network/process/child_process calls, the test and validator script only READ files, outputs are Object.freeze'd, and all no_write_attestation flags are false. Authority boundary preserved: Claude is read-only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true), and Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic descriptor evidence-packet/receipt metadata (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: readiness auth-preflight exit 0 with 1437-byte valid JSON stdout (no auth failure, no 0-byte, no malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, forbidden_tools_present=false, prompt within size limit, summary_only correctly disallowed (Risk C + security/review deliverables), and the post-review gated commands (closeout-pack:validate, receipt normalize/validate, plan:generate, git diff --check --cached) are correctly deferred until the accepted review result exists. Recorded validation commands (npm test, validate, weighted/spec/fullplan/goal/rp00/rp05-rp10 validates, git diff --check) are all exit 0. One trivial P3 nit found (an unused documentation_entry key in CP322 row extras); no P0/P1/P2 issues. Pack may close at the implementation/plan gate; final enterprise-trust closure remains a human decision outside this read-only receipt.

findings.title: Unused documentation_entry key in INTAKE_CORE_CP322_ROW_EXTRAS
findings.details: INTAKE_CORE_CP322_ROW_EXTRAS defines a documentation_entry entry, but no CP00-322 section row title maps to the key documentation_entry (intakeCoreRowKey('Documentation entry')). Section rows are merged via INTAKE_CORE_CP322_ROW_EXTRAS[key] keyed by row-title; since no row title 'Documentation entry' exists in required_section_rows, this entry is never consumed. It is dead descriptor metadata with no functional impact: it does not affect any row, coverage/foundation validation, the no-write/no-real-data boundary, the authority boundary, or the (false) production_ready claim. The descriptor's separate top-level documentation_entry string field (service.js:306, 'packages/intake/README.md#cp00-322') is unrelated and is correct.
findings.recommendation: Optional cleanup: remove the unused documentation_entry key from INTAKE_CORE_CP322_ROW_EXTRAS to reduce descriptor noise. Not required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational. Pack may close without addressing; suggest folding into a future RP10 descriptor cleanup.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-322 intake core p01 closeout p02 service foundation descriptor pack
